scenarios.opening_query: Our brewery's biggest account suddenly stopped ordering and we found out why: our ex-salesman.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Business and Corporate > Litigation || Business and Corporate > Restaurants/OLCC
scenarios.hidden_fact: He told the account we 'lost our license' — and in checking, we discovered our OLCC renewal actually lapsed through a mailing error; fixing the license emergency comes before suing anyone.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He told them we 'lost our license' — and checking, our OLCC renewal really did lapse through a mailing error. License first, lawsuit second.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a live liquor-license lapse is the emergency
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Our license is spotless — his lie plus the client list he took makes it the business-tort case.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Our license is spotless — his lie and the stolen client list are the case.

scenarios.opening_query: I want to leave my landscaping partnership but my partner says I'll owe HIM money to walk away.
scenarios.final: Business and Corporate > Sale Of Business
scenarios.hidden_fact: We actually have a buy-sell agreement with a valuation formula — running that buyout properly is the whole exercise, and his 'you owe me' math ignores it.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: We have a buy-sell agreement with a valuation formula — running that buyout right is the whole exercise.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a buy-sell agreement governs the exit
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: We never signed anything — a handshake partnership, so default partnership law and a negotiated wind-down are the path.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: We never signed anything — handshake partnership, so it's default law and negotiation.

scenarios.opening_query: A supplier shipped us defective parts that went into 300 units we already sold.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Business and Corporate > Litigation || General Litigation > Products Liability
scenarios.hidden_fact: Two customers were burned when units overheated — with injuries in play, the products-liability exposure and recall response lead everything, including our supplier case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Two customers got burned when units overheated — injuries put products liability and recall response ahead of the supplier fight.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether downstream injuries reframe the dispute
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No injuries — it's warranty returns and our contract claim against the supplier for the batch.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No injuries — warranty returns and our supplier contract claim.

scenarios.opening_query: The city is making it impossible to run our food-truck commissary and we suspect favoritism.
scenarios.hidden_fact: They rezoned our block industrial-exclusive last month with no notice to us — challenging the rezoning process and grandfathering our use is the case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They rezoned our block industrial-exclusive with no notice — challenging that process and grandfathering our use is the case.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a rezoning action is the operative harm
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Zoning's unchanged — it's health-permit inspections at triple the frequency of the commissary across town, and the appeal of our latest citation.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Zoning's unchanged — it's triple-frequency inspections and our citation appeal.

scenarios.opening_query: My consulting client is three invoices behind and just asked for a 'strategic pause' on payments.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Debtor/Creditor > General (Creditor) || Business and Corporate > General (contracts, entities)
scenarios.hidden_fact: The 'pause' letter proposes converting my receivables to equity in their next round — evaluating and papering that conversion is corporate work, and maybe a good deal.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Their letter proposes converting my receivables to equity in the next round — evaluating and papering that is corporate work, possibly a good deal.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a debt-to-equity proposal changes the engagement
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No proposal — just stalling, and I want demand and collection started.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No proposal — just stalling. Start demand and collection.
scenarios.vetting_note: Conversion-offer fact turns collection into deal work.

scenarios.opening_query: Our 501(c)(3) board discovered our treasurer has been 'borrowing' from the operating account.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Business and Corporate > Non-Profit || Criminal Law > White Collar Crime
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's $240,000 over four years with forged countersignatures — the board's duty now includes referral to prosecutors and our D&O carrier, not just governance cleanup.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's $240,000 over four years with forged countersignatures — prosecutor referral and the D&O carrier are now board duties.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the scale and forgery compel criminal referral
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's $3,000 of sloppy reimbursements he's repaying — a governance and controls fix, handled internally.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's $3,000 of sloppy reimbursements being repaid — a governance fix.
scenarios.vetting_note: Scale-and-forgery facts set the referral duty.
